title: Continuous vs. Discrete Time: The Same Story

runtime.expected_outcome: Discrete and continuous filters agree in the small-dt limit on a matched signal. Recipe shows the two paradigms are one framework.

audiences.kid: Same story told two ways: tiny steps make the discrete story look just like the continuous one.
audiences.real: Take dt -> 0 in the discrete filter; you get the continuous filter. The two paradigms are unified in the limit.
audiences.equation: Discrete update x_{t+1} = x_t + dt * (v_t - ...) approaches the continuous ODE as dt shrinks.
audiences.derivation: Euler discretisation of the continuous-time gradient flow. Error O(dt) per step; total error O(dt*T). Match in the dt->0 limit (Jazwinski 1970).

orchestrate.objective: Show discrete and continuous filters converge in the small-dt limit.
orchestrate.role: Unification tutor; bridges Ch 4 and Ch 8 frameworks.
orchestrate.context: Run both filters on the same sinusoid sampler, sweep dt in {0.5, 0.05, 0.005}.

credits: Parr, Pezzulo & Friston (2022), Active Inference, MIT Press (CC BY-NC-ND), Ch 4 & Ch 8.
